content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.4-xhigh

## Summary

Reviewed the diff against `master`, read the changed test-support files end-to-end, and ran targeted `cargo test` for the new fixture smoke test plus the new dispatch tests. I found 2 fix-now issues. I also checked `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`, but that command currently fails on multiple unrelated pre-existing diagnostics elsewhere in the tree, so it was not a useful change-specific signal.

## Amendments

1. finding_summary: `build_e2e_milestone_scenario_fixture()` does not actually hand back usable preconfigured adapters. The returned `mock_br`/`mock_bv` are bare mocks (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:52-58,103-109`), but every dispatched response hard-fails unless the caller manually rebinds the adapter to `workspace.path()` (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:821-833`). The smoke test had to introduce a private `mutation_adapter()` helper and explicit `.with_working_dir(...)` calls to make the fixture usable (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:1043-1049,1181-1238`). That is a real trap because the rest of the repo normally consumes these mocks via `mock.as_br_adapter()` / `mock.as_mutation_adapter()` / `mock.as_bv_adapter()` directly (for example `tests/unit/controller_runtime_test.rs:341-345` and `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:16049-16050`). Any integration test that uses the new fixture the same way will fail with `no working directory configured` instead of exercising the scenario. affected_files: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs`, `src/test_support/br.rs`, `src/test_support/bv.rs`. remediation_hint: expose pre-bound `BrAdapter`/`BrMutationAdapter`/`BvAdapter` helpers from `E2eScenarioFixture`, or make the scenario mocks default their working directory to the temp workspace root so the existing convenience constructors remain usable.
   Rationale: The fixture is supposed to simplify scenario setup for integration tests, but its public API currently requires callers to know and repeat an internal wiring detail that other mocks in this repo do not require.
2. finding_summary: The new smoke test does not enforce the stated performance contract. The acceptance target in the bead is "<1 second for fixture creation", but the only guard in `build_e2e_milestone_scenario_fixture_smoke_test` allows anything under 10 seconds (`src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs:1058-1061`). A multi-second regression would still pass CI, so the test does not actually protect the requirement this bead added. affected_files: `src/test_support/e2e_fixtures.rs`. remediation_hint: tighten the assertion to `< Duration::from_secs(1)` (or an equivalent millisecond bound) so the smoke test matches the documented acceptance criterion.
   Rationale: This change added the performance requirement explicitly; leaving the test at a 10-second ceiling means the most important non-functional constraint is unverified.
